overview: A relatively quiet day on bpf-next, with two series landing. Mahe Tardy's long-running series (v10) to add a bpf_icmp_send kfunc reached the mailing list alongside a verifier fix from Woojin Ji that improves how scalar zero spills are tracked during stack reads.

patches.title: bpf: add bpf_icmp_send kfunc
patches.author: Mahe Tardy
patches.summary: This patch (1/5 of the series) introduces a new kfunc, bpf_icmp_send, that lets BPF programs generate and send ICMP messages from within a cgroup_skb hook. Reaching version 10, the series has gone through substantial iteration to handle corner cases like recursion and missing routes. The accompanying selftests cover IPv4 and IPv6 cgroup_skb attachment, recursion guards, and the no-route error path, giving broad confidence in the new interface.

patches.title: bpf: Preserve scalar zero spills for stack reads
patches.author: Woojin Ji
patches.summary: This verifier patch fixes a correctness gap where scalar values that are known to be zero get their type information lost after being spilled to the stack and later read back at a variable offset. Without the fix, the verifier forgets the zero constraint and may reject otherwise valid programs or produce imprecise type tracking. The companion selftest (patch 2/2) adds targeted coverage for the affected stack-read patterns.
